steps.title: Find the short height
steps.content:
Triangle $ABC$ is right, with hypotenuse $AB=\sqrt{61}$ and leg $AC=6$. Use the Pythagorean theorem.
$$BC^2+6^2=(\sqrt{61})^2$$
$$BC^2+36=61$$
$$BC=5$$

steps.title: Find the scale factor
steps.content:
Since $AC=6$ and $CE=18$, the full base is
$$AE=6+18=24$$
The similar-triangle scale factor from $ABC$ to $AED$ is
$$\frac{AE}{AC}=\frac{24}{6}=4$$

steps.title: Scale the height
steps.content:
The corresponding height also scales by $4$.
$$ED=4\cdot BC=4\cdot5=20$$

steps.title: Calculate the area
steps.content:
Use base $AE=24$ and height $ED=20$.
$$\frac{1}{2}\cdot24\cdot20=240$$
The answer is 240.
